title: MCP Snippet Tools for CRUD operations on code snippets
purpose: Provides tools for creating, reading, updating, deleting, and exporting code snippets stored as .snippet files in session folders.
module_context: Exports interfaces for snippet operations, schemas for input validation, and helper functions for anchor-based extraction and language detection. Defines URL building logic for API calls.

methods.name: buildUrl
methods.normalized_params: (path: string, project: string, session: string, extraParams?: Record<string, string>): string

steps.content: Construct a URL using the base API endpoint, provided path, and project/session parameters.
Append project and session as URL search parameters.
If extra parameters are provided, add them as additional URL search parameters.
Return the fully constructed URL string.

methods.name: findAnchorLine
methods.normalized_params: (lines: string[], anchor: string, label: string, filePath: string): number

steps.content: Trim the anchor string for consistent matching.
Iterate through each line to find matches of the trimmed anchor string.
If no matches are found, throw an error indicating the anchor was not found in the file.
If multiple matches are found, throw an error showing all matching lines and their context.
Return the index of the single matching line.

methods.name: extractWithAnchors
methods.normalized_params: (fileContent: string, filePath: string, startAt?: string, endAt?: string, maxLines?: number): string

steps.content: Validate that filePath and fileContent are provided.
Normalize line endings to Unix-style.
Split the content into lines.
If the file appears minified (single line) and anchors are specified, throw an error.
Determine the start and end indices based on anchor strings or default to full file.
If the end index is before the start index, throw an error about incorrect anchor order.
If the extracted range exceeds the maximum lines limit, throw an error.
Return the extracted lines joined back into a string.

methods.name: handleCreateSnippet
methods.normalized_params: (project: string, session: string, name?: string, content?: string, sourcePath?: string, startLine?: number, endLine?: number, groupId?: string, groupName?: string, startAt?: string, endAt?: string, maxLines?: number): Promise<CreateSnippetResult>

steps.content: If sourcePath is provided, read the file content and detect the language from the file extension.
If anchor-based extraction is specified, extract content using the anchor helper function.
If legacy line-based extraction is used, slice the content based on start and end lines.
If groupId or groupName is provided, inject them into the JSON content.
If content is raw code, wrap it in a JSON envelope with language detection.
Make a POST request to the API endpoint to create the snippet with the final name and content.
If the API request fails, throw an error with the response message.
Return the success status and ID from the API response.

methods.name: handleUpdateSnippet
methods.normalized_params: (project: string, session: string, id: string, content: string): Promise<UpdateSnippetResult>

steps.content: Attempt to parse the incoming content as JSON.
Fetch the existing snippet data from the API.
If the existing snippet is JSON, merge the incoming content with the existing envelope.
If the existing snippet is not JSON, wrap the incoming content in a new envelope.
Make a POST request to the API endpoint to update the snippet with the merged content.
If the API request fails, throw an error with the response message.
Return the success status and ID from the API response.

methods.name: handleGetSnippet
methods.normalized_params: (project: string, session: string, id: string): Promise<GetSnippetResult>

steps.content: Make a GET request to the API endpoint to fetch the snippet by ID.
If the API request fails, throw an error indicating the snippet was not found.
Parse the JSON envelope from the snippet content to extract code and metadata.
If annotations are present, inject them as comment lines above their annotated ranges.
Return the snippet data with parsed code, language, file path, and last modified timestamp.
Make a GET request to the API endpoint to list all snippets for the project and session.
If the API request fails, throw an error with the response message.
Return the list of snippets from the API response.

methods.name: handleDeleteSnippet
methods.normalized_params: (project: string, session: string, id: string): Promise<DeleteSnippetResult>

steps.content: Make a DELETE request to the API endpoint to remove the snippet by ID.
If the API request fails, throw an error with the response message.
Return the success status from the API response.

methods.name: handleExportSnippet
methods.normalized_params: (project: string, session: string, id: string, format?: string): Promise<ExportSnippetResult>

steps.content: Fetch the snippet data using the get snippet handler.
If the export format is JSON, serialize the snippet data into a JSON string.
If the export format is text, return the snippet content directly.
Return the success status, content, and format from the export operation.
